qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the "Multinode (by core + requeue)" Slurm script for Ansys Fluent jobs?
qa_pairs.answer: The "Multinode (by core + requeue)" Slurm script is designed to run Ansys Fluent jobs across multiple cores and includes an automatic requeue mechanism. It will resubmit the job up to a specified number of times (e.g., `#SBATCH --array=1-5%1`) if it fails due to a license shortage or a simulation issue. If the job completes successfully, it cancels any remaining requeue attempts, otherwise, it indicates if more attempts will be made or if all attempts have failed.

qa_pairs.question: How does the "Multinode (by core + requeue)" Slurm script configure parallel execution for Ansys Fluent on different clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: The script configures parallel execution by setting `NCORES` to the total number of cores requested (`$SLURM_NTASKS`) and uses Intel MPI. If the job runs on a single node, Fluent uses shared memory parallel (`-pshmem`). For multiple nodes, it uses distributed memory parallel: `-peth` for the Nibi cluster and `-pib` for other clusters. In both multinode cases, it uses a dynamically generated machine file (`/tmp/machinefile-$SLURM_JOB_ID`). Special environment variables are also set for Narval and Nibi clusters depending on the Ansys version.
